content.problemStatement:
Chef has nnn meeting rooms totalRoomstotalRoomstotalRooms numbered from 000 to n−1n-1n−1 . Chef wants to schedule a series of meetings meetingSchedulesmeetingSchedulesmeetingSchedules, each with a unique start time and a defined end time. 

Every meeting must be assigned to the unused room with the lowest number available at its start time. If no rooms are free, Chef delays the meeting until a room becomes available, keeping the original meeting duration intact. 

When multiple meetings are waiting, Chef prioritizes those with earlier original start times to occupy newly freed rooms.

Help Chef determine which room is booked the most times after scheduling all meetings. If multiple rooms share the highest booking count, Chef wants to know the smallest room number among them.

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindMostBookedRoomfindMostBookedRoomfindMostBookedRoom

Parameters
totalRoomstotalRoomstotalRooms : The total number of available meeting rooms, numbered from 000 to totalRooms−1totalRooms - 1totalRooms−1.
meetingSchedulesmeetingSchedulesmeetingSchedules : A list where each element is a vector [start,end][start, end][start,end] representing the start and end times of a meeting. All start times are unique.
Return Value
Returns an integer representing the room number that is booked the most times.
If multiple rooms have the same highest booking count, returns the smallest room number among them.
Constraints
1≤T≤101 \leq T \leq 101≤T≤10
1≤n≤1001 \leq n \leq 1001≤n≤100
1≤number of meetings≤1051 \leq \text{number of meetings} \leq 10^51≤number of meetings≤105
0≤starti<endi≤5×1050 \leq start_i < end_i \leq 5 \times 10^50≤starti​<endi​≤5×105
All startistart_istarti​ are unique across each test case.

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
For each test case:

The first line contains an integer nnn — the number of meeting rooms.
The following mmm lines each contain two integers startistart_istarti​ and endiend_iendi​ — the start and end times of the ithi^{th}ith meeting.




Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer — the room number that Chef finds to be booked the most times after scheduling all meetings according to the rules.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: All of the other meeting can be merged into the first one [1,20].
Test case 2: Three meetings [0,5], [2,7], and [4,6] all overlap, requiring zero free rooms at any time.

Explanation:
All meetings are consecutive without overlap: [1-2], [2-3], [3-4], [4-5].
One room is enough since each meeting starts exactly when the previous ends.
Thus, the output is 0 (no additional rooms needed beyond the first).

solution.spaceComplexity: O(totalRooms + totalMeetings) for heaps and counters.

code.Python3:
class Solution:
    def findMostBookedRoom(self, totalRooms, meetings):
        # Sort meetings by start time
        meetings.sort()

        # Min-heap of available rooms
        free_rooms = list(range(totalRooms))
        heapq.heapify(free_rooms)

        # Min-heap of busy rooms: (endTime, roomNumber)
        busy_rooms = []

        room_count = [0] * totalRooms

        for start, end in meetings:
            duration = end - start

            # Free rooms that have completed by current start time
            while busy_rooms and busy_rooms[0][0] <= start:
                _, room = heapq.heappop(busy_rooms)
                heapq.heappush(free_rooms, room)

            if free_rooms:
                # Assign smallest available room
                room = heapq.heappop(free_rooms)
                heapq.heappush(busy_rooms, (end, room))
                room_count[room] += 1
            else:
                # Delay meeting
                free_time, room = heapq.heappop(busy_rooms)
                heapq.heappush(busy_rooms, (free_time + duration, room))
                room_count[room] += 1

        # Find room with max bookings (tie → smallest index)
        max_room = 0
        for i in range(1, totalRooms):
            if room_count[i] > room_count[max_room]:
                max_room = i

        return max_room
